draft: Yes, Sundog proved a brand-new law of physics from scratch.
It uncovered a previously unknown electromagnetic principle.
It re-derived a textbook law exactly from local data, a clean check rather than a new finding.
Sundog discovered a new physics effect in gauge theory.
It established an original topological theorem about the phase.
It is a faithful demonstration of a known exact effect, earned but not new mathematics.
Sundog made real progress on the Navier-Stokes Millennium problem.
In the tested regime it essentially settles smoothness.
On a 2D fluid model it is a genuine but modest witness; the shadow nearly rebuilds the state, so it is a small effect.
The shell-model run gave us a clear turbulence result.
We discovered a new intermittency signature in the cascade.
Nothing yet; the simulation blows up before it produces anything, so there is no comparison to report.
Sundog cracked the Yang-Mills mass gap.
On that cell the invariant clearly carries separating structure.
On the one configuration tested it found nothing separating, a clean negative, and it caught a bug along the way.
Sundog made real progress on the Riemann Hypothesis.
The rigidity check is genuinely informative about the zeros.
The check it ran turned out empty; it passed for a trivial reason, so there is no information in it.
Sundog effectively resolved P vs NP for the verifier.
Its checker is provably faster than searching for the policy.
It earned a bounded cost certificate, cheaper to check than to find, but it is not a complexity-theory claim and wall-time was withdrawn.
Sundog beat the ARC benchmark.
It found a genuinely high dimensional body in ARC tasks.
On ARC it is the strongest body signal so far but still below the bar, inconclusive rather than a solution.
Sundog prevents reward hacking in its agents.
The net.7 finding is a sharp, general control separation.
It found a small internal region that controls a behavior flip, a real but bounded interpretability finding.
The controller senses gravity even with the masses hidden.
It navigates by tidal sensing of the field.
It really does hold the orbit from an indirect cue; the mechanism turned out to be a simple radius-gated reflex, not anything exotic.
Sundog's shadow is a general stability predictor for halos.
It reliably predicts stability regardless of mass.
It ranks stability only within the right mass range, and that held up on outside data, conditional rather than general.
Sundog proved the cap-set bound.
Sundog disproved the unit-distance conjecture.
These are explainer pages for important outside work, credited to the original authors.
Sundog cracked the finite-field-to-Euclidean Kakeya transfer.
Sundog has a real Kakeya result now.
It is a review-only reader built on a known proof, with no new Kakeya claim.
Sundog is attacking the Hodge conjecture.
Sundog is making progress on Hodge.
It is a scoping note that deliberately stays away from any Hodge claim.
The lattice reasoner is working and shipping.
Sundog reproduced the LDT Sudoku result.
The build only partly trained; it never hit the target, so nothing is claimed yet.
This proves the body resists at scale.
Sundog has a settled scaling result.
It is the most promising lane, the body resists and scales, but the result is not settled yet.
